sections.paragraph:
fut. יֵקַל, יֵקַלּוּ.-

(1) to be light (Ethiop. ቀለለ፡ id., قَلِيلُ, ቀሊል፡ light [not heavy]), see Hiphil. Figuratively-

(2) to be diminished (Arab. قَلَّ), Gen 8:11, קַלּוּ הַמַּיִם מֵעַל הָאָרֶץ “the waters were diminished (i.e. had flowed away) from off the earth;” verse .

(3) to be despised, contemned, Job 40:4 Nah 1:14. Compare קָלָה No. II. Inf. used as a noun, קֹל ignominy, disgrace, Jer 3:9 (where קֹל is regarded by others as the same as קֹול).

(4) to be swift, fleet (if indeed this be not the primary signification, compare גָּלַל to roll swiftly), 2Sa 1:23 Hab 1:8 Job 7:6 9:25 .

Niphal נָקַל and נָקֵל, fut. יִקַּלּוּ Isa 30:16.-

(1) to be light. עַל־נְקַלָּה lightly (leichthin), Jer 6:14 8:11 . Followed by a dat. of pers. to be easy to any one, Pro 14:6 2Ki 20:10.

(2) to be of little account, little, followed by בְּעֵינֵי 1Sa 18:23. Impers. נָקֵל מִן is it a light thing that, Isa 49:6 Eze 8:17.

(3) to be lightly esteemed, to be despised, 2Sa 6:22 Gen 16:4, Gen 16:5.

(4) to be swift, Isa 30:16.

Piel קִלֵּל to curse, to execrate, 2Sa 16:7 followed by an acc. Gen 8:21 12:3 Exo 21:17 Lev 19:14 20:9 once followed by בְּ Isa 8:21. קִלֵּל לֹו reflex. to curse oneself, i.e. to bring a curse upon oneself, 1Sa 3:13, “because he knew כִּי מְקַלְלִים לָהֶם בָּנָיו that his sons had brought a curse upon themselves.”

Pual, to be cursed, Isa 65:20 Job 24:18. Part. one who is accursed, Psa 37:22.

Hiphil הֵקֵל, inf. הָקֵל, fut. יָקֵל.-

(1) to make light, to lighten-(a) followed by an acc. of the thing and מֵעַל of pers. to lighten and cast away any thing from any one, 1Ki 12:10 1Sa 6:5.-(b) without the accusative, Exo 18:22, הָקֵל מֵעָלֶיךָ “lighten from off thee,” sc. the burden, business, make thy business lighter; Jon 1:5.-(c) followed by מֵעַל of the thing, 1Ki 12:4, הָקֵל מֵעֲבֹדַת אָבִיךָ “lighten (somewhat) from the servitude of thy father,” i.e. remit somewhat of the servitude which thy father imposed upon us; verse 1Ki 12:9.

(2) to reckon lightly, to despise, 2 Sam. 19:44 Eze 22:7 to bring to contempt, Isa. 8:23 .

Pilpel קִלְקַל-

(1) to move to and fro, to shake together, Eze 21:26. Arabic قَلْقَلَ Ethiopic እንቀልቀለ፡ to be moved.

(2) to make smooth, to polish; hence to sharpen, Ecc 10:10. The notion of smoothness (which originally does not differ from that of lightness) is also found in the adj. קָלָל.

Hithpalpel, to be moved, shaken together, Jer 4:24.

Derivatives, קַל, קָלָל, קְלָלָה, קְלֹקֵל, קִיקָלֹון, pr.n. קַלַּי, and-
